P4_control.note: context only; >=0.50 => model not needed for the majority

findings.headline: The 12B's format failures are cheaply repairable. A single flash pass (reasoning OFF) recovered 57/63 (90%) of the stock arm's dead-on-arrival candidates into valid skeletons, 24 (38% of corpus) into GENUINE live survivors, at median claim-fidelity 1.0 — the 12B's arrow-of-time reasoning preserved verbatim, only JSON/predicate syntax fixed. Deterministic control recovered only 16%, so the model does real work on the majority.
findings.flash_vs_pro: TIE on recovery (both 57/63 = 90.5%); pro won survival narrowly (28 vs 24 survivors). No reason to pay for pro on repair — flash is the cost-effective seat (43.8k vs pro 43.2k tokens/arm).
findings.reasoning_off_beat_reasoning_on: The killed first run (reasoning ON) recovered 46/63 at 125k flash tokens; reasoning OFF recovered 57/63 at 43.8k. For pure format repair, reasoning both COST 3x AND lowered recovery (the model overthought and mutated well-formed content). Confirms docs/TOKEN_ECONOMY.md beyond generation: repair wants thinking disabled too.
findings.residual_gap: Recovery 90% but survival only 38% of corpus: ~33 recovered skeletons parse yet still self-refute — mostly forbidden cases whose own predicate fails on the candidate. Repairing SYNTAX does not repair a semantically self-defeating forbidden case; that is a generation-quality limit of the 12B, not a format one.

caveats: Corpus is the committed 12B stock/compact malformed candidates (63, all from A-stock); single provider, n=1 per arm.

claim_fidelity is token-set Jaccard vs a regex-salvaged claim; 1.0 median means the repaired claim's token set matched the salvageable original — strong evidence of preservation, not a semantic-equivalence proof.
survival is mechanical-criticism survival only (no judge in the loop); it measures criticizability recovered, not answer quality.
Repaired skeletons were assessed in-memory, not written back into a run root — this experiment measures recoverability, it does not graft the repairs onto the 12B log.

reasoning_on_run_superseded.note: first live run left DeepSeek thinking ON (bug); flash 46/63 recovered, 24 survived, 125330 tokens. Superseded by this reasoning-off run; recorded, not deleted (append-only record).

token_accounting_note: arm 'tokens' is the SHARED meter snapshot (cumulative across arms); 'marginal_tokens' is each arm's own spend. flash 43,812 + pro 43,216 = 87,028 total, well under the 250k cap.
